Notes: Approach shoe TR to clean after top roping kiddos all morning

Started climbing at 8:15, finished climbing at 4pm. Back to the car at 5.  8h 45m car to car, 7h 45m climbing. Surprisingly good climbing and surprisingly not a TON of choss! Still plenty of choss, but less than we were expecting. Found a couple fixed pitons and some really old tat. AGoot rained on a little bit at the top of the first pitch, but we stayed pretty dry and the day ended up amazing! Great day out :)

Partner(s): Kimberly O’., Arthur Br., Holly Fr.
Notes: Hangdogged the crap out of this one! Fell one move shy of getting through the crux first try and rope stretch took me right back to the ground, so I had to fire the whole thing again. Had to hang several more times through the rest because of the pump! Fun line :)

Oh my god thank GOODNESS I climbed this for my last climb instead of Snott Girlz, it's in a whole 'nother league! Some sweet crack climbing with interesting, fun movement :) Saw some ground squirrels chilling out in the talus below from the top of P3! I led 1&2, 3, and 5

First bolted line at EPC! I led 2 & 4. I thought 1 was hardest because of the polish, 2 was most fun, and I can see how 4 can be seen as the crux pitch but it was so well bolted it didn't feel too bad at all. Arthur got buzzed by a hawk while following pitch 2!
Nuthin' to say

Circumnavigated the icy glacier on rock to the right which added a few hours. Pitched out a spooky crevasse crossing with a thin snow bridge but neither of us fell in. Made it all the way to the false summit before turning back due to it being late. So close!!
Had to turn back on the steep ice because there was no snow and we reached impassable seracs and steep ice and we just weren't prepared for legit ice climbing
